claims_verified.claim: kickoff.sh captures a non-flag token after --new as BRIEF and excludes it from REST
claims_verified.evidence: scripts/kickoff.sh:63-85, confirmed by manual bash trace: BRIEF captured, REST=--skip-clarify --reason hi when run with (--new --skip-clarify --reason hi)

claims_verified.claim: --brief is forwarded only to the --phase new invocation, never to clarify/design/loop
claims_verified.evidence: scripts/kickoff.sh:102 (`--phase new ${BRIEF:+--brief "$BRIEF"}`) vs lines 108/114/122 which only expand ${REST[@]+...}; test_kickoff_forwards_brief_only_to_new in tests/test_kickoff_wiring.py:47-64 asserts this statically

claims_verified.claim: ${BRIEF:+--brief "$BRIEF"} correctly preserves spaces in the brief and expands to nothing when BRIEF is empty
claims_verified.evidence: manual bash test: BRIEF="hello world with spaces" produced 2 separate argv tokens [--brief][hello world with spaces]; BRIEF="" produced ARGC=2 with no --brief token

claims_verified.claim: empty brief (--brief "") behaves as no brief in _phase_new (falsy check)
no-brief prompt is byte-identical to pre-change SPEC_AUTHOR_PROMPT
claims_verified.evidence: orchestrator/run.py:100-106 appends SPEC_AUTHOR_BRIEF_BLOCK only `if brief`; test_default_author_spec_no_brief_is_byte_identical in tests/test_run_cli.py asserts equality with SPEC_AUTHOR_PROMPT.format(...)

claims_verified.claim: Deps.author_spec signature and all call sites/tests updated consistently to accept brief
claims_verified.evidence: grep across orchestrator/run.py and tests/test_run_cli.py shows all lambdas/functions updated to 4-arg signature; no stale 3-arg caller found

claims_verified.claim: full gate passes on this branch
claims_verified.evidence: ruff check . -> 'All checks passed!'; basedpyright -> '0 errors, 2133 warnings, 0 notes'; pytest -n auto -q -> '1113 passed, 9 skipped, 3 warnings'

test_assessment: AC1 (interactive session with brief in prompt, no clarify crash) covered by test_default_author_spec_threads_brief_into_prompt plus the kickoff.sh routing test and the fixed REST exclusion (manually traced). AC2 (no-brief byte-identical) covered by test_default_author_spec_no_brief_is_byte_identical and test_new_mode_no_brief_seed_and_prompt_unchanged. AC3 (seed contains brief; empty-authoring guard fires against brief-inclusive seed) covered by test_new_mode_with_brief_seeds_spec_and_threads_it and test_new_mode_empty_authoring_guard_fires_with_brief. AC4 (empty brief == no brief) covered by test_new_mode_empty_brief_same_as_no_brief for run.py; kickoff.sh's ${BRIEF:+...} empty-expansion behavior is only exercised by manual verification, not a repo test, but is a one-line, low-risk shell idiom. AC5 (flag-looking token after --new is not swallowed as brief) is only indirectly protected by the kickoff.sh capture logic (`[[ "${ARGS[$i]}" != -* ]]`); there is no dedicated automated test for this branch, though it is straightforward and I verified it manually. AC6 (unit tests: brief-only-to-new routing, _phase_new seeding/threading, no-brief regression guard, empty-guard-fires-with-brief) is fully covered by the new tests in both test files. Every new/changed function (_default_author_spec, _phase_new, kickoff.sh parsing loop) has at least one exercising test; the guard's error path (rc==2) is explicitly tested with and without a brief.
residual_risks: kickoff.sh's --new argument capture and the empty-BRIEF expansion are verified only by static text assertions in tests plus my own manual bash trace, not by an automated test that actually executes kickoff.sh end-to-end; a future shell refactor could silently break this without failing the test suite.
AC5 (flag-looking token after --new not treated as brief) has no dedicated repo test, relying on manual verification during this review.
